name: Who is responsible if a tool malfunctions and causes a serious injury?

mainEntity.name: Who is responsible if a tool malfunctions and causes a serious injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your employer is responsible for workers\' comp benefits, but the manufacturer or a maintenance company may be liable for additional damages if the equipment was defective or poorly serviced.
acceptedAnswer.description:
When equipment malfunctions at work, the cause of the failure determines who is liable. While your employer provides workers\' compensation benefits regardless of why the tool broke, a third party—such as the equipment manufacturer, a repair contractor, or a rental company—could be held liable in a separate personal injury lawsuit.

This matters because workers\' comp only pays for bills and basic wages. A lawsuit against a negligent manufacturer can provide compensation for your physical pain, emotional distress, and the full loss of your quality of life.

Key factors include:
- Was the equipment maintained according to manufacturer specs?
- Did a third-party technician recently service the tool?
- Was there a known design flaw in that specific equipment model?
- Did the employer bypass any built-in safety features?

Determining liability requires a forensic investigation of the failed equipment. Often, an attorney will hire an engineer to inspect the tool to prove it was defective, which can significantly increase your total recovery.
